patch.value: Telemetry first-run prompt CI guard is inverted: CI=true does not skip, only CI=false/0/no/off does

shouldSkipTelemetryPrompt (src/core/telemetry/consent.ts) reuses TELEMETRY_PROMPT_DISABLE_VALUES ('0','false','no','off') via parseBooleanLike for the CI env check. parseBooleanLike(process.env.CI) is therefore true only when CI is explicitly falsy — i.e. the prompt is skipped when CI=false and NOT skipped when CI=true. Masked in practice because real CI shells are non-TTY (the TTY guard fires first), but an interactive shell with CI=true would still prompt. Fix: use a truthy-value check (e.g. CI is set and not in the disable set) for the CI guard. Found while extending tests/unit/telemetry-consent.spec.ts (test asserts current behavior with a comment).

value.author: cli-fixes-agent
value.text: Truthiness rules for the CI guard (new isTruthyEnvValue, separate from parseBooleanLike which only matches the disable set): the prompt is skipped iff CI is SET to a value that is non-empty after trim and not in {0,false,no,off} (case-insensitive) - so CI=true, CI=1, CI=yes, and any other CI=<token> skip. CI unset, CI="" (empty/whitespace, treated as unset per common CI conventions), and the explicit falsy spellings do NOT skip. PM_TELEMETRY_PROMPT keeps its existing opt-out semantics (disable values suppress the prompt) - only the CI check changed. tests/unit/telemetry-consent.spec.ts updated: the inverted assertion (CI=false skips) replaced with CI=true/CI=1 skip assertions plus a new test that CI=false and CI="" still prompt interactively.

patch.value: CI=true (or CI=1) skips the telemetry first-run prompt; CI=false or empty behaves as unset.

Previously the guard reused the disable-value set, so only CI=false/0/no/off skipped and CI=true did not (masked by the non-TTY guard in real CI); now truthy CI values skip and falsy/empty values fall through to the interactive prompt.

Fixed the inverted CI guard in shouldSkipTelemetryPrompt: a new isTruthyEnvValue check skips the first-run prompt when CI is set to any non-empty value outside {0,false,no,off}; CI unset/empty/falsy behaves as not-CI. PM_TELEMETRY_PROMPT opt-out semantics unchanged. telemetry-consent.spec.ts assertions flipped to the fixed behavior plus a new test that CI=false and CI="" still prompt.
